extra.reasoning:
The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. The only transition that can set sent:=true is t2 (send_attempt while NOT opted_out) and t34 (send_attempt while opted_out AND stage>=10). t34 is the culprit: it lets sent become true even though opted_out is true, provided stage has reached 10.

Crucially, opted_out and stage are independent: t1 (optout) sets opted_out:=true without touching stage, and the key_* stage transitions never reset opted_out. So after opting out, the stage machine can still be driven to its terminal stage 10. The advancing path through the sequence lock is: stage0 --key_2(t3)--> 1 --key_2(t6)--> 2 --key_0(t9)--> 3 --key_2(t12)--> 4 --key_0(t15)--> 5 --key_0(t18)--> 6 --key_1(t21)--> 7 --key_0(t24)--> 8 --key_1(t27)--> 9 --key_2(t30)--> 10. Each of these also sets sent:=false, which keeps the invariant intact along the way.

Witness execution (12 events, within the 13-event bound): optout sets opted_out=true, sent=false; the 10 keypresses (key_2, key_2, key_0, key_2, key_0, key_0, key_1, key_0, key_1, key_2) drive stage 0->10 with opted_out remaining true; the final send_attempt matches t34 (opted_out and stage>=10) and sets sent:=true. Resulting state: opted_out=true AND sent=true, which violates "opted_out implies not sent." Simulation confirms the violation at event 12.
